danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing deity's followers rendered on isometric map with distinct visual separation from player followers
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player's civilization; must be eliminated to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Swamps, volcanoes, lava terrain, water barriers rendered in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Natural obstacles that impede follower movement and settlement; can be weaponized by either side

danger_signals.visual_form: Slider bar measurement of divine power shown on command interface
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced ability to cast divine intervention spells; vulnerability window

danger_signals.visual_form: Elevated or depressed land with no building space in isometric view
danger_signals.meaning: Prevents follower settlement and expansion; indicates unsafe or unusable territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Level ground space in isometric perspective, larger areas for larger houses
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for follower settlement and population growth

safety_signals.visual_form: Full or high slider bar on command interface
safety_signals.meaning: Access to full divine power arsenal; strong defensive/offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual density of player followers in settled areas on map
safety_signals.meaning: Numerical superiority and path to victory; safe position relative to enemy

safety_signals.visual_form: Terrain shaped and modified by player interventions (raised/lowered land)
safety_signals.meaning: Territory under player influence; safe from natural hazards

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player followers visually distinguished from enemy followers on isometric map
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Settled flat terrain with player-constructed houses; modified landscape through divine intervention

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct follower rendering to differentiate from player faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy settlements on landscape; terrain shaped by opposing divine intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse follower population; limited landscape modification visible; few constructed buildings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers wander terrain seeking flat building space; limited divine powers available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Growing follower density; more extensive terrain reshaping; multiple settled areas visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers actively construct houses on available flat land; access to intermediate divine powers (earthquakes, floods, swamps)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game / High mana victory state
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense follower populations on both sides; extensively modified landscape; approach to endgame conflict
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to knight transformation and catastrophic powers; preparation for final battle between factions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy follower density visibly increasing; enemy terrain expansion visible on map; player settlement shrinkage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy divine interventions visible (landscape changes, hazard creation); need for defensive divine powers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert, rock, lava, snow and ice rendered distinctly in isometric view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Follower development and settlement patterns vary by landscape type; affects game strategy and pacing

environmental_storytelling.example: Raised or lowered land serves as natural border between player and enemy territories; water barriers and swamps define territorial separation

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Visible concentration of houses and followers indicates which deity has stronger presence in a region

environmental_storytelling.example: Volcanoes, earthquakes, and floods leave visual marks on terrain showing history of divine conflict
Overhead tabletop presentation reinforces player role as deity observing civilization from divine vantage point

environmental_storytelling.technique: Open book interface as world representation
environmental_storytelling.example: World depicted as pages in book emphasizes game world as constructed/authored realm under player control

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Wanders terrain; seeks barriers as movement limits
Encounters blank flat land and builds house; house size correlates with available land area
More houses create more followers; increases mana level for player
Ordinary followers converted to more powerful knights; indicates late-game aggressive positioning
Visual distinction allows player to identify enemy expansion vectors and threat zones

notes: Populous communicates world-state primarily through isometric terrain visualization and follower density clustering. Political situation is manifested through visible separation of follower populations and terrain control. Danger is signaled through enemy presence, hazardous terrain types, and mana depletion. The god-game paradigm positions visual communication around divine intervention effects: terrain reshaping, follower transformation, and catastrophic events. The 'tabletop' interface frame reinforces player omniscience while making faction control graphically legible.
